H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) leads Over‑stimulation with 66 % positive. Qualitative feedback (n=29) shows high music love and settle times split between 5 and 10 minutes. YouTube reach is highest at 30 views, though retention data are missing. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, under‑estimating observed efficacy. Distribution is right‑skewed.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Most people feel calmer quickly and really enjoy the track; it’s the most discovered protocol.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) maintains solid performance; 65 % positive. Qualitative respondents (n=21) highlight noticeable improvement and a 10‑minute settle pattern. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon prediction is not directly listed but aligns with modest mean. Drift (0.780) indicates relative stability.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners notice improvement after about ten minutes; music is liked but not loved.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) sits between L1 and L2, with 64 % positive. Qualitative data (n=23) reveals use for sleep despite over‑stimulation label, and balanced rhythm opinions. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon does not list a prediction; observed mean is consistent with series trend. Drift (0.784) is moderate.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Works well for many, even when used for sleep, with balanced rhythm feedback.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) exhibits a right‑skewed distribution with 63 % positive responses. Qualitative feedback (n=23) reports mostly moderate to noticeable improvement and settle times around 10 minutes. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views), suggesting strong efficacy yet limited exposure. AutoGluon predicts 7.84, closely matching the observed mean. No bimodality evident; variance reflects diverse listener contexts.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners generally feel a noticeable calm after about ten minutes, and many love the music.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows a tighter cluster centered on scores 7‑8. Qualitative data (n=20) indicates mixed settle times (5 vs 20 minutes) and mixed music opinions. YouTube views are 6, providing negligible reach. AutoGluon does not flag a large prediction gap. Slightly lower mean may reflect groove‑style differences.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Most people report a moderate lift in mood, though some need a bit longer to feel the effect.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) displays a flatter distribution with modest variance. Twenty respondents note a 10‑minute settle time, but four required the full session, hinting at slower entrainment for some. No YouTube metrics available. AutoGluon prediction aligns with observed mean. Drift similarity (0.695) suggests analytic framing has shifted modestly.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: The protocol works for many, but a few listeners need the full session before settling.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) has the lowest HEALING mean, with 53 % positive responses as expected for acute distress. Despite lower scores, 24 qualitative responses emphasize significant improvement (8 respondents). YouTube reach is 6 views. AutoGluon predicts a higher 7.96, indicating latent potential. Distribution is moderately left‑skewed.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Even in crisis moments, users see some improvement, though scores are lower than other Healing protocols.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) improves modestly over L1; 55 % positive. Qualitative sample (n=16) reports mixed music sentiment and settle times split between 5 and 10 minutes. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon cross‑bucket recommends X‑ME‑L1/L2, suggesting overlap with anxiety zones. Drift (0.662) signals analytic shift.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: A small boost is common, with mixed feelings about the music and a quick settle for many.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) mirrors L2 mean but with highest variance in the crisis set. Qualitative data notes rapid settlement for 7 respondents yet 4 disliked the music. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.96, again above observed. Drift (0.711) is moderate.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Fast settling is reported, but some listeners dislike the music, hinting at a split audience.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) tops Sleep & Rest with 65 % positive. Qualitative sample (n=15) reports rapid settlement (5‑minute range) and high music appreciation. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, matching observed efficacy. Distribution shows gradual rise toward higher scores.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Good for sleep; several settle within five minutes and love the melody.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) offers the tightest variance in Sleep, 67 % positive. Qualitative responses (n=15) note a caregiver‑heavy listener mix and occasional “too busy” rhythm perception. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon prediction aligns. Drift (0.802) is relatively low.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Consistent calming effect; a few caregivers note the rhythm feels a bit busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) maintains strong performance with 64 % positive. Qualitative data (n=22) shows balanced settle times and mixed rhythm opinions. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon does not list a prediction; observed mean is typical for Sleep zone. Drift (0.785) moderate.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Steady sleep aid with mixed settle times and rhythm impressions.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) is the weakest Focus protocol, 56 % positive. Qualitative responses (n=23) report “slightly busy” rhythm concerns. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus zone, indicating a gap between model expectation and observed score.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus benefits are modest, and some find the rhythm slightly busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) improves markedly over L1, 66 % positive. Qualitative data (n=17) shows balanced rhythm and caregiver involvement. YouTube views are 4. AutoGluon prediction aligns with observed mean.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Noticeable focus gains; rhythm feels balanced for most listeners.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) tops the entire library with 71 % positive and a bimodal distribution (spike at score 10). Qualitative feedback (n=29) confirms strong improvement and mixed rhythm opinions. YouTube views are 15, the highest in THRIVING. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, closely matching observed performance.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Excellent focus for many, with a notable number scoring the highest possible.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) records the lowest positive rate in Creative Flow (53 %). Qualitative feedback (n=21) notes many “slight improvement” ratings and fast settle times. YouTube views are 2, indicating negligible reach. AutoGluon does not list a prediction; observed mean aligns with modest efficacy.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow gains are modest; listeners often feel only slight improvement.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) tightens variance with 58 % positive. Qualitative sample (n=15) highlights significant improvement and strong music love. YouTube views are 2. AutoGluon prediction not provided; mean is modestly higher than L1.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Slightly better flow, with some users reporting significant improvement.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) achieves the highest Creative Flow mean with low variance. Qualitative data (n=25) is larger than outcome sample, showing balanced settle times. YouTube views are 7. Drift similarity (0.663) suggests analytic shift despite small n.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Strong creative boost for most, with consistent responses despite a small sample.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) shows tight variance and 64 % positive. Qualitative sample (n=23) indicates long settle times (20 minutes for many). YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon prediction not listed; observed mean is solid for Maintenance.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners feel balanced after a longer session; music is well received.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) has the widest variance and lowest mean in Maintenance (59 % positive). Qualitative data (n=16) notes mixed improvements and a notable “studying” activity pattern. YouTube views are 4. Drift (0.785) moderate.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed balance results; some find it harder to settle quickly.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) shows 71 % positive despite small sample. Qualitative responses (n=19) include four listeners who did not settle, flagging potential entrainment issues. YouTube views are 3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance, a modest upward gap.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Generally positive but a few listeners never feel settled.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) attains 54 % positive; qualitative feedback (n=22) highlights strong music love and long settle times (up to 20 minutes). YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 (low zone), markedly higher than observed, indicating cross‑bucket potential.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood lifts are modest; many listeners love the music but need longer time.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) modestly improves over L1, 64 % positive, with mixed music opinions. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon prediction not listed; observed mean aligns with series expectations.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Slightly better mood; listeners are split on how much they like it.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) shows the lowest mean among Mood & Energy, 56 % positive. Qualitative responses (n=25) note moderate improvement and mixed rhythm opinions. YouTube views are 4. Drift (0.627) moderate.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mood boost is modest; listeners report mixed rhythm experiences.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) shows the lowest positive rate in TRANSFORMING (45 %). Qualitative data (n=17) paradoxically reports many “significant” improvements, hinting at response bias. YouTube views are 5. AutoGluon predicts 7.88 for motivation, far above observed.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation gains are low overall, despite some users reporting strong improvement.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) continues the low motivation trend (43 % positive) with modest variance. Qualitative responses (n=21) feature “trying to sleep” activity, perhaps indicating off‑target use. YouTube views are 7. Drift (0.585) moderate.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Motivation stays modest; listeners often use it while trying to sleep.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) reverses the motivation trend with a high mean but very high variance and the lowest drift similarity (0.507). Qualitative data (n=17) is limited but shows mixed improvement ratings. YouTube views are 7. AutoGluon predicts 7.88, aligning with observed mean but variance remains a concern.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Higher motivation scores for some, but responses are very spread out.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) yields 54 % positive; qualitative feedback (n=25) includes two “made things worse” reports, a warning flag. Music is well‑liked, but rhythm opinions split. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for low zone, higher than observed.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mixed clarity effects; a couple of users felt worse, while others liked the music.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) improves slightly over L1, 56 % positive, with strong “very settling” rhythm ratings. YouTube views are 12, the highest among TRANSFORMING entries. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 (mindclarity), exceeding observed mean.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Better clarity for many, with a calming rhythm that many love.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) records the lowest mean and highest variance across all protocols, 50 % positive. Qualitative comments note mixed music reception and slow settle times. YouTube views are 8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a sizable gap suggesting population mismatch.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results vary widely; some love it, others find it ineffective.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across 1,000 listener outcomes the data reveal distinct efficacy patterns for the three series.
• HEALING shows the highest average (6.98) and the tightest variance, consistent with its descent architecture.
• THRIVING matches HEALING in mean (6.96) but displays the widest score spread, especially in Focus & Clarity.
• TRANSFORMING trails with the lowest mean (6.59) and greatest variability, reflecting its ascending‑arc design.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level performance aligns with design intent while level effects dominate within sub‑groups.
• HEALING protocols cluster tightly around a 7.0 mean, indicating reliable nervous‑system regulation.
• THRIVING holds the top mean in Focus & Clarity (T‑FC‑L3) yet the lowest in Creative Flow, showing strong level‑dependent gradients.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits the lowest mean and highest std, with Motivation & Drive protocols pulling the series down.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge from expected patterns and merit deeper study.
• X‑MC‑L3 (mean 5.70, std 2.45) is far below its series average and shows a bimodal distribution.
• T‑FC‑L3 displays a pronounced upper‑tail (10 % of responses at score 10) creating a bimodal shape.
• H‑CR‑L1‑L3 have low observed means (≈6.4) yet AutoGluon predicts scores near 8, suggesting latent efficacy.
• X‑MD‑L3 shows the lowest drift similarity (0.507), indicating unstable analytical framing.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
The data suggest several concrete avenues for protocol refinement.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Investigate population splits in crisis protocols H‑CR‑L1/L2/L3 and consider separate adviser routing or L2 variants.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Extend the hold phase for focus protocols (e.g., T‑FC‑L1) to improve entrainment for slower responders.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Test a gentler ascent curve for motivation protocols X‑MD‑L1/L2, possibly adding an L2.5 intermediate level.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Create an L2.5 or revised L3 variant for mind‑clarity X‑MC‑L3 to bridge the performance gap.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Boost YouTube discovery for high‑scoring but low‑view protocols (e.g., H‑AX‑L1) via targeted thumbnails and description cues.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Increase sample sizes for drift‑high protocols (X‑MD‑L3, X‑ME‑L2, X‑ME‑L3) to stabilise predictive modeling.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals highlight widespread analytical change and limited clustering.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: All 30 protocols show similarity < 0.85, indicating notable drift across runs.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient embeddings to form reliable clusters.
• Confidence note: Embedding‑derived insights are currently low confidence; additional runs and richer embeddings are needed.

by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Consistent calming effect; a few caregivers note the rhythm feels a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Steady sleep aid with mixed settle times and rhythm impressions.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Creative flow gains are modest; listeners often feel only slight improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Slightly better flow, with some users reporting significant improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Strong creative boost for most, with consistent responses despite a small sample.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus benefits are modest, and some find the rhythm slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Noticeable focus gains; rhythm feels balanced for most listeners.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Excellent focus for many, with a notable number scoring the highest possible.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Listeners feel balanced after a longer session; music is well received.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Mixed balance results; some find it harder to settle quickly.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Generally positive but a few listeners never feel settled.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mixed clarity effects; a couple of users felt worse, while others liked the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Better clarity for many, with a calming rhythm that many love.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Results vary widely; some love it, others find it ineffective.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation gains are low overall, despite some users reporting strong improvement.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Motivation stays modest; listeners often use it while trying to sleep.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Higher motivation scores for some, but responses are very spread out.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood lifts are modest; many listeners love the music but need longer time.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Slightly better mood; listeners are split on how much they like it.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Mood boost is modest; listeners report mixed rhythm experiences.
